questions.q:
Consider the following reactions
A→P1;B→P2;C→P3;D→P4
The order of the above reactions are a, b, c and d,respectively. The following graph is obtained when log[rate] vs.log[conc.] are plotted:

  
Among the following, the correct sequence for the order of the reactions is :
questions.solution:
Rate =K[Conc]order 

logRate =logK+ order× log[conc]
so slope of graph is order of reaction. greater the slope greater is order of reaction so order of reaction ⇒"c<a<b<d''
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: Which one of the following given graphs represents the variation of rate constant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mo>)</mo></math> with temperature <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi><mo>)</mo></math> for an endothermic reaction?
questions.solution:
Relation between rate constant (k) and absolute temperature (T):

Arrhenius equation: k =A e-EaRT

The value of rate constant (k) will increase exponentially with rise in temperature for all type of reactions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Match List-I with List-II.

  List-I   List-II
A Cds+2NiOH3s→CdOs+2NiOH2s+H2Ol I Primary battery
B ZnHg+HgOs→ZnOs+Hgl II Discharging of secondary battery
C 2PbSO4s+2H2OI→Pbs+PbO2s+2H2SO4aq III Fuel cell
D 2H2g+O2g→2H2Ol IV Charging of secondary battery

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
questions.solution:
(a)Cds+2NiOH3s→CdOs+2NiOH2s+H2Ol 
Discharge of secondary Battery

(b)ZnHg+HgOs→ZnOs+Hgl 
(Primary Battery Mercury cell)

(c) 2PbSO4s+2H2Ol→Pbs+PbO2s+2H2SO4aq Charging of secondary Battery

(d) 2H2g+O2g→2H2Ol- Fuel cell

Mercury cell, suitable for low current devices like hearing aids, watches, etc. consists of zinc - mercury amalgam as anode and a paste of HgO and carbon as the cathode. The electrolyte is a paste of KOH and ZnO. The electrode reactions for the cell are given below:

Anode: ZnHg+2OH-→ZnOs+H2O+2e-

Cathode: HgO+H2O+2e-→Hgl+2OH-

Another important secondary cell is the nickel-cadmium cell which has longer life than the lead storage cell but more expensive to manufacture.

The overall reaction during discharge is:

Cds+2NiOH3s→CdOs+2NiOH2s+H2Ol

Fuel cell using H2 and O2 produces electricity.

Cathode: O2g+2H2Ol+4e-→4OH-aq

Anode: 2H2g+4OH-aq→4H2Ol+4e-

Overall reaction being:

2H2g+O2g→2H2Ol

(ii) Cell reaction of lead storage battery during recharge -

The reverse of oxidation at anode during discharge is the reduction reaction at cathode during recharging.

Pbaq2++2e-→Pbs

PbSO4s→Pbaq2++SO4aq2-

Final Reaction: PbSO4s+2e-→Pbs+SO4aq2-
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn></math> moles of argon and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn></math> moles of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>PCl</mi><mn>5</mn></msub></math> are introduced into an evacuated flask of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn></math> litre capacity at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>610</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math>. The system is allowed to equilibrate. At equilibrium, the total pressure of mixture was found to be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mi>atm</mi></math>. The <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></msub></math> for the reaction is [Given : <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>082</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi></math> atm <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>mol</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> ]
questions.solution:
PCl5=5 mole

Ar=4 mole

PTotal=9×0.082×610100=4.5 atm

PPCl5=5×4.59=2.5; PAr=4×4.59=2

PCl5⇌PCl3+Cl22.5-P P     P

Ptotal=2.5-P+P+P+PAr=6

P=1.5

Kp=1.5×1.51=2.25
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The standard electrode potential of M+/M in aqueous solution does not depend on
options: Hydration of a gaseous metal ion
Ionisation of a solid metal atom
Ionisation of a gaseous metal atom
questions.solution:
The stability of M+ ions in aqueous medium depends on three factors: (i) Enthalpy of atomisation (ii) First ionisation enthalpies of isolated gaseous atom (iii) Hydration enthalpy. 

An element in M+ state in aqueous medium is stabler if the electrode potential ( M+/M ) value of more negative. Down the group, the tendency to form M+ ion increases. In this process, ionisation of solid metal is not involved in this process.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
For lead storage battery pick the correct statements

A. During charging of battery, PbSO4 on anode is converted into PbO2

B. During charging of battery, PbSO4 on cathode is converted into PbO2

C. Lead storage battery consists of grid of lead packed with PbO2 as anode

D. Lead storage battery has ~38% solution of sulphuric acid as an electrolyte

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution: The lead storage battery is as shown in diagram. Lead storage battery consists of lead anode and a grid of lead packed with lead oxide(PbO2) as cathode, a 38% solution of H2SO4 is used as an electrolyte. During charging of battery PbSO4 deposited on cathode is converted in PbO2 and PbSO4 deposited on anode is converted into Pb. The electrolyte used in battery is H2SO4 which is about 38% by mass.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q:
A solution is 0.1M in Cl- and 0.001M in CrO42-.
Solid AgNO3 is gradually added to it Assuming that the addition does not change in volume and Ksp(AgCl)=1.7×10-10M2 and
KspAg2CrO4=1.9×10-12M3.

Select correct statement from the following:
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>AgCl</mi></math> precipitates first because its <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mi>sp</mi></msub></math> is high.

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Ag</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>CrO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> precipitates first as its <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mi>sp</mi></msub></math> is low.

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Ag</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>CrO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> precipitates first because the amount of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Ag</mi><mo>+</mo></msup></math> needed is low.

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>AgCl</mi></math> will precipitate first as the amount of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Ag</mi><mo>+</mo></msup></math> needed to precipitate is low.
questions.solution:
i Ag+ required to ppt AgCls

Ksp=IP=Ag+Cl-=1.7×10-10

Ag+=1.7×10-9

ii Ag+ required to ppt Ag2CrO4s

Ksp=IP=[Ag+]2CrO4-2=1.9×10-12

Ag+=4.3×10-5

Ag+ required to ppt AgCl is low so AgCl will ppt 1st.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q:
Ka1,Ka2 and Ka3 are the respective ionization constants for the following reactions a,b and c.

(a) H2C2O4⇌H++HC2O4-

(b) HC2O4-⇌H++HC2O42-

(c) H2C2O4⇌2H++C2O42-

The relationship between Ka1, Ka2 and Ka3 is given as
questions.solution:
H2C2O4⇌H++HC2O4-Ka1

H2C2O4-⇌H++C2O42-Ka2

H2C2O4⇌2H++C2O42-   Ka3

On adding equation 1 and equation 2 we get equation 3. So, Ka3=Ka1×Ka2.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The Plot of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>pH</mi></math>-metric titration of weak base <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mi>OH</mi></math> vs strong acid <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HCl</mi></math> looks like
questions.solution:
Titration curve of NH4OH vs HClWB+SA

At the very beginning of the curve, the pH starts by falling quite quickly as the acid is added, but the curve very soon gets less steep. This is because a buffer solution is being set up composed of the excess ammonium hydroxide and the ammonium chloride being formed.

Notice that the equivalence point is now somewhat acidic ( a bit less than pH 5), because pure ammonium chloride isn't neutral. However, the equivalence point still falls on the steepest bit of the curve. That will turn out to be important in choosing a suitable indicator for the titration.

For the above titration the indicator suitable is methyl orange
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>200</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>01</mn><mi>MHCl</mi></math> is mixed with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>400</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>01</mn><msub><mi>MH</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math>. The <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>pH</mi></math> of the mixture is
questions.solution:
pH of a mixture of HCl+H2SO4 has to calculated

The number of equivalents of HCl is equal to 0.02 equivalents

The number of equivalents of H2SO4 given is equal to 0.008 equivalents

Hydrogen ion concentration of the resultant mixture is equal toH+=0.01×200+0.01×2×400600

=2+8600=10600=160

pH=-log160

=1.78

questions.q: Strong reducing and oxidizing agents among the following , respectively, are
questions.solution:
ECe4+/Ce3+=+1.74 V

Lanthanoids have +3 oxidation state as stable oxidation state. Hence, in their +4 oxidation state, they act as an oxidising agents and  +2 oxidation state, they act as reducing agents.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: During the reaction of permanganate with thiosulphate, the change in oxidation of manganese occurs by value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math>. Identify which of the below medium will favour the reaction.
options: Both aqueous acidic and faintly alkaline
questions.solution:
In neutral or weakly alkaline solution oxidation state of Mn changes by 3 unit. The reaction between permanganate and thiosulfate is a redox reaction, in which permanganate acts as an oxidizing agent and thiosulfate acts as a reducing agent.

MnO4⊝+S2O3-2→MnO2+SO4-2

This ionic mechanism is favoured in neutral aqueous medium.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q:
Henry's constant (in kbar) for four gases α,β,γ  and δ in water at 298 K is given below :

αβγδKH5022×10-50.5

(density of water =103 k gm-3 at 298 K ) This table implies that :
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> has the highest solubility in water at a given pressure

The pressure of a <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>55</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn></math> molal solution of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">δ</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>250</mn></math> bar
questions.solution:
(1) From Henery's law P=kHX

Higher the value of kH smaller will be solubility so γ is more soluble.

(2) Though solubility of gas will decrease with increase in temperature but this conclusion can not be drawn from the given table.

(3)For

δ⇒Pδ=KHδ.Xδ

=0.5×103×55.5555.55+100018=250bar.

(4)For γ

pγ=KHγ ×xγ

=2×10-255.555.5+100018=10-2bar

questions.q: At <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>35</mn><msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></msup><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>,</mo></math> the vapour pressure of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo></math> is <math><mn>512</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> Hg and that of acetone is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>144</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>.</mo></math> A solution of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> in acetone has a total vapour pressure of <math><mn>600 </mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> . The false statement amongst the following is:
options: Raoult's law is not obeyed by this system

a mixture of <math><mn>100</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><mn>100</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi></math> acetone has a volume <math><mo><</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mn>200</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi></math>
<math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> and acetone are less attracted to each other than to themselves
heat must be absorbed in order to produce the solution at <math><msup><mrow><mn>35</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>
questions.solution:
P observed =600
 
P Cal = P A 0 x A +P B 0 x B

=512x+344−344x

=168x+344

=512 (If x=1 for maximum value)

Thus,   P cal < P observed

Thus' the process is deviated from Raoult's law, showing positive deviation from Raoult's law.

ΔH>0 , i.e., is absorbed

If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi></math> is the density of the solution in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>gmI</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is the molarity and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is the mole fraction of the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>2</mn><mtext>nd </mtext></msup></math> component, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> can be expressed as :

questions.q:
If a rocket runs on a fuel C15H30 and liquid oxygen, the weight of oxygen required and CO2 released for every litre of fuel respectively are :
(Given : density of the fuel is 0.756 g/mL)
questions.solution:
density=massvolume

mass of C15H30=d×V=0.756×1000=756 gram

C15H30l+452O2g⟶15CO2g+15H2O

Moles of C15H30 = 756210 Moles of O2 used= 452756210 mole  Moles of CO2 formed=15756210

WO2=45275621032=2592 gram

WCO2=2376 gram
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Hemoglobin contains <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>34</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of iron by mass. The number of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Fe</mi></math> atoms in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of hemoglobin is (Given : Atomic mass of Fe is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>56</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi><mo>,</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></msub></math> in<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>022</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>23</mn></msup><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>mol</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
questions.solution:
Mass of haemoglobin = 3.3g

Mass of iron atoms = 0.34100×3.3=0.01122g

56 g of iron contains = 1 mole of iron or 6.023 × 1023 atoms

Number of moles of atoms=Mass atomic mass

Number of moles of 0.01122g of iron is

Number of moles = 0.01122g56g=0.00020 moles

Number of atoms =Number of moles ×avogadro number

No. of Fe atoms

 =0.00020×6.022×1023=1.2046 × 1020
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: A commercially sold conc. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HCl</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>35</mn><mo>%</mo><mo> </mo><mi>HCl</mi></math> by mass. If the density of this commercial acid is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>46</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>/</mo><mi>mL</mi></math>, the molarity of this solution is : (Atomic mass : <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Cl</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>35</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>amu</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mi>amu</mi></math>)
questions.solution:
Molarity M=x×d×10mB

=35×1.46×1036.5=14 M

mB= molecular mass of solute

d= density of the solution in g/L

x=% solute by mass.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>250</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> solution of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi></math>-glucose in water contains <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of carbon by weight. The molality of the solution is nearest to (Given: Atomic Weights are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi><mo>;</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>12</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi><mo>;</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>16</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi></math>)
questions.solution:
C6H12O6→Glucose

Given: %C=10.8=mass of Cmass of solution×100

10.8×250100= mass of C⇒ Mass of C=27gm

mass of Cmass of glucose=72180mass of glucose = 27×18072

∴ mass of glucose =67.5gm

∴ moles of glucose =67.5180 = 0.375 moles

Mass of solvent =250-67.5gm=182.5gm

∴ Molality =moles of solutemass of solvent (kg) = 0.3750.1825=2.055≈2.06
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q:
Consider the reaction

4HNO3l+3KCls→Cl2g+NOClg+2H2Og+3KNO3s

The amount of HNO3 required to produce110.0 g of KNO3 is

(Given : Atomic masses of H,O,N and K are 1,16,14 and 39, respectively.)
questions.solution:
4HNO3l+3KCls→Cl2g+NOClg+2H2Og+3KNO3g

                        HNO3                          KNO3Weight            Xgm                             110gmmoles                 x63                              110101

As per the stoichiometric equation4 moles of HNO3→3 moles of KNO3

1→34

x63→34×x63=110101

x=110×63×4101×3=91.5gm
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q:
Cs+O2g→CO2g+400 kJ

Cs+12O2g→COg+100 kJ

When coal of purity 60% is allowed to burn in presence of insufficient oxygen, 60% of carbon is converted into ' CO' and the remaining is converted into 'CO2'.

The heat generated when 0.6 kg of coal is burnt is
questions.solution:
CS+O2g→CO2g+400 kJ

1 mole

Cs+12O2g→COg+100 kJ    ...2

0.6Kg×1000

=600gm

Mass of 60% pure coal

=600×60100 (Pure Carbon)

=360gmNumber of moles=36012=30 moles of pure carbon

Moles of 60% pure Carbon converted into CO2=30-30×60100

=12 mole and

Moles of 60% pure carbon converted in to CO=30×60100=18 mole

Energy generated during 2 equation =18×100

=1800 kJ

Energy generated during 1st reaction.

=12×400

=4800

Total =1800+4800=6600 kJ

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Production of iron in blast furnace follows the following equation

Fe3O4s+4COg→3Fel+4CO2g

when 4.640kg of Fe3O4 and 2.520kg of CO are allowed to react then the amount of iror (in g) produced is :

[Given: Molar Atomic mass gmol-1:Fe=56

Molar Atomic mass gmolm-1:O=16

Molar Atomic mass gmolm-1:C=12]
questions.solution:
Moles of Fe3O4=4.640×103232=20

Moles of CO=2.52×10328=90

So limiting Reagent =Fe3O4

So moles of Fe formed =60

Weight of Fe=60×56=3360gms
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: Outermost electronic configuration of a group <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>13</mn></math> element, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi><mo>,</mo></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mn>1</mn></msup><mo>.</mo></math> The electronic configuration of an element of p-block period-five placed diagonally to element, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi></math> is:
questions.solution: The element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Ga</mi></math> and the diagonal element of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>5</mn><mtext>th </mtext></msup></math> period is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mmultiscripts><mi>Sn</mi><mprescripts/><mn>50</mn><none/></mmultiscripts></math> having outer electronic configuration will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>[</mo><mi>Kr</mi><mo>]</mo><mn>5</mn><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>4</mn><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi></mrow><mn>10</mn></msup><mn>5</mn><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>.</mo></math>
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
According to Bohr's atomic theory:
(A) Kinetic energy of electron is ∝Z2n2.
(B) The product of velocity (v) of electron and principal quantum number n, 'vn'∝Z2.
(C) Frequency of revolution of electron in an orbit is ∝Z3n3.
(D) Coulombic force of attraction on the electron is ∝Z3n4.

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
According to the Bohr's atomic theory, the kinetic energy of an electron is directly proportional to the square of the atomic number and inversely proportional to the square of the principal quantum number. 

(A) KE=13.6z2n2eVatom⇒KEαz2n2

(B) speed of e-αzn

∴  v×nαz

(C) Frequency of revolution of e-=v2πr

∴ Frequency αz2n3

(D) Coulombic force of attraction on the electron is directly proportional to the square of the atomic number and inversely proportional to the fourth power of the principal quantum number. 

 F=kq1q2r2=kze2r2  rαn2z

⇒ Fαzn2z2

⇒  Fαz3n4
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Given below are two statements

Statement I : According to Bohr’s model of hydrogen atom, the angular momentum of an electron in a given stationary state is quantised.

Statement II : The concept of electron in Bohr’s orbit, violates the Heisenberg uncertainty principle. In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
options: Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> is incorrect but Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> is correct
Both Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> and Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> are incorrect

Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> is correct but Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> is incorrect
questions.solution: According to Bohr's postulates the angular momentum in stationary orbit is quantised and is equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>nh</mi><mo>/</mo><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></math> is the principal quantum number of the orbit. Bohr's model of the atom assumes fixed orbits and trajectories for the electron. Simultaneously known orbits and trajectories violate the Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q:
At 25 °C and 1 atm pressure, the enthalpies of combustion are as given below:

Substance H2 C (graphite) C2H6g
ΔcHΘkJmol-1 -286.0 -394.0 -1560.0

The enthalpy of formation of ethane is
questions.solution:
Given

(i) C2H6g+72O2g⟶2CO2g+3H2OlΔHcomb°=-1560.0 kJ/mole

(ii) Cs+O2g⟶CO2gΔHcomb°=-394.0 kJ/mole

(ii) H2g+12O2g⟶H2OgΔHcomb°=-286.0 kJ/mole

Target 2Cs+3H2g⟶C2H6gΔHrx°=ΔHf°C2H6, g

ΔHr°=ΔHc°reactant-ΔHc°Product

=2×-394+3-286--1560

=-788-858+1560

=-86.0KJ/mole
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: What happens when methane undergoes combustion in systems A and B respectively?
options:
System A System B
Temperature rises Temperature remains same

System A System B
Temperature remains same Temperature rises

System A System B
Temperature falls Temperature remains same

System A System B
Temperature falls Temperature rises
questions.solution:
For an adiabatic free expansion of an ideal gas, the gas is kept in an insulated container and then permitted to enlarge in a vacuum. Since there is no external pressure for the gas to enlarge against, the work done by or on the system is zero. Since this process does not involve any exchange of heat transfer or work, heat will not escape and temperature of system will rise.

For diathermic container, heat will escape the container and hence temperature of container will remain same.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q:
Match List - I with List - II.

  List-I   List-II
A Spontaneous process I ΔH<0
B Process with ΔP=0,ΔT=0 II ΔGT,P<0
C ΔHreaction III Isothermal and isobaric process
D Exothermic Process IV [Bond energies of molecules in reactants] -
[Bond energies of product molecules]

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
questions.solution:
(A) For a spontaneous process ΔGT,P<0

(B) ΔP=0→ Isobaric process(pressure is constant in this process) ΔT=0→ Isothermal process(Temperature is constant in this process).

(C) ΔHreaction=(Σ Bond energies of reactants)-
(Σbond energies of products)

(D) ΔH<0 is for exothermic reaction
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
